perturbation.original_text: "Efficiency" means the quotient of E/F, where E = the electricity produced by the Portfolio, measured in BTUs (British Thermal Units) at a conversion rate of 3,412 BTUs per kWh, and F = the fuel consumed by the Portfolio, measured in BTUs on a Lower Heating Value basis.
perturbation.changed_text: "Efficiency" means the quotient of E/F, where E = the electricity produced by the Portfolio, measured in BTUs, and F = the fuel consumed by the Portfolio, measured in BTUs.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the specific conversion rate (3,412 BTUs per kWh) introduces an ambiguity in the calculation of 'Efficiency'. This omission creates an internal contradiction because later sections, such as Exhibit B (Efficiency Bank Operation Example Calculation), rely on this specific conversion rate to perform calculations. Without a defined BTU/kWh conversion, there would be uncertainty in the contract, rendering the examples and warranty clauses unenforceable. This create an in-text contradiction in the sections were the efficiency is being calculated.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Exhibit B to MASTER OPERATION AND MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT B to MASTER OPERATION AND MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT EFFICIENCY BANK OPERATION EXAMPLE CALCULATION Exhibit B-1 Source: BLOOM ENERGY CORP, DRS/A (on S-1), 3/21/2018 Efficiency Bank -MOMA Exhibit B Efficiency Bank Operation Example Calculation 2014 Assumptions Number of active Systems 150 Nameplate capacity 200 Hours in the year 8760 Look back period 30 Days BTUs/kWh 3,412 LHV to HHV conversion 1.107 Actual power performance 96% One-Month Efficiency analysis One-Month Efficiency Warranty 50% Actual system efficiency 56% Maximum MMbtu 156,643 Actual MMbtu 139,860 MMbtu to be drawn from Efficiency Bank — MMbtu to be deposited into Efficiency Bank 16,783 Efficiency Bank beginning balance 104,429 Change 16,783 Efficiency Bank ending balance [***] [***] Confidential Treatment Requested Source: BLOOM ENERGY CORP, DRS/A (on S-1), 3/21/2018
perturbation.justification: The removal of "at a conversion rate of 3,412 BTUs per kWh" in the definition of "Efficiency" directly contradicts Exhibit B, which explicitly uses the value 'BTUs/kWh 3,412' for its calculations. Without this defined conversion rate in the main definition, the calculations in Exhibit B become uncertain and the contract becomes unenforceable. This is an 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' as defined.

perturbation.original_text: "Minimum Power Product" means the aggregate Nameplate Capacity of the Bloom Systems in the Portfolio in kW for the applicable Power Performance Warranty Period multiplied by (1) eighty-five percent (85%) when this term is used for the One-Month Power Performance Warranty or (2) ninety-five percent (95%) when this term is used for the One-Year Power Performance Warranty. An example of a calculation of the Minimum Power Product is set forth in Appendix B.
perturbation.changed_text: "Minimum Power Product" means the aggregate Nameplate Capacity of the Bloom Systems in the Portfolio in kW for the applicable Power Performance Warranty Period multiplied by a percentage. An example of a calculation of the Minimum Power Product is set forth in Appendix B.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the specific percentage values (85% and 95%) used in the calculation of 'Minimum Power Product' creates an in-text contradiction with the subsequent sections where the Power Performance Warranty is discussed, and the example calculations are provided in Appendix B. The phrase 'multiplied by a percentage' is too vague, and the deletion of the specific percentages makes the warranty clauses unenforceable. The lack of defined percentages creates ambiguity and would hinder enforcement. Specifically the One-Month Power Performance Warranty and One-Year Power Performance Warranty now have no way to be calculated
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.6 Power Performance Warranty. During the Warranty Period, Operator shall determine (i) for each full calendar month (the "One- Month Power Performance Warranty Period") within five (5) Business Days after the end of such month and (ii) for the most recent Look Back Period (the "One-Year Power Performance Warranty Period", and, together with the One-Month Power Performance Warranty Period, each a "Power Performance Warranty Period"), whether the Bloom Systems in the Portfolio during such Power Performance Warranty Period have delivered to the Interconnection Point the Minimum kWh during such Power Performance Warranty Period (the "Power Performance Warranty"). If such Power Performance Warranty calculation indicates that the Actual kWh of the Bloom Systems was less than the Minimum kWh during such Power Performance Warranty Period, then Operator shall so notify Owner in writing of the basis of its determination and Owner may make a claim under Section 2.5. An example of a Power Performance Warranty calculation for purposes of a Section 2.5 claim is attached as Appendix D.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific percentage values (85% and 95%) used in the calculation of the 'Minimum Power Product'. This creates a contradiction with Section 2.6 (Power Performance Warranty) which refers to the 'Minimum kWh' during the Power Performance Warranty Period, and Appendix D, which provides an example of a Power Performance Warranty calculation. Without the specific percentages defined in the 'Minimum Power Product' definition, calculating the 'Minimum kWh' as required by Section 2.6 becomes impossible, rendering the warranty clause unenforceable. The omission of the percentages introduces ambiguity where certainty is required, and this breaks compatibility between the definition of 'Minimum Power Product' and the Power Performance Warranty clause, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Operator shall cause Owner (and such additional parties as Owner designates in writing) to be named additional insured(s), must be written as primary policy not contributing to or in excess of any policies carried by the Owner, and each contain a waiver of subrogation endorsement, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Owner, in favor of the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: Operator shall cause Owner (and such additional parties as Owner designates in writing) to be named additional insured(s).
perturbation.explanation: The deletion of the phrase 'must be written as primary policy not contributing to or in excess of any policies carried by the Owner, and each contain a waiver of subrogation endorsement, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Owner, in favor of the Owner' from Section 9.22 creates ambiguity. The insurance requirements are not clearly defined, the obligations of the insurer are made unclear due to the deletion of the waiver. This change introduce uncertainty because without the specific requirements, Owner cannot ensure that the insurance policies obtained by Operator adequately protect Owner's interests. This is contradicting the fact, that there is still requirement for the owner to be insured in the text but they remove the whole purpose of it, making it impossible to enforce it properly because there are key parts missing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 9.22 Insurance. At all times during the Term without cost to Owner, Operator shall maintain in force the following insurance, which insurance shall not be subject to cancellation, termination or other material adverse changes unless the insurer delivers to Owner 31 Source: BLOOM ENERGY CORP, DRS/A (on S-1), 3/21/2018 written notice of the cancellation, termination or change at least thirty (30) days in advance of the effective date of the cancellation, termination or material adverse change: (a) Worker's Compensation Insurance as required by the laws of the state where Operator's facilities are located; (b) Employer's liability insurance with limits not less than One Million Dollars ($1,000,000); and (c) Commercial General Liability Insurance, including bodily injury and property damage liability including premises operations, contractual liability endorsements, products liability and completed operations with limits not less than Five Million Dollars ($5,000,000). Operator shall cause Owner (and such additional parties as Owner designates in writing) to be named additional insured(s).
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the Operator's insurance policy must be primary and contain a waiver of subrogation. The changed text removes these critical requirements. The contradicted text in Section 9.22 states that the Operator shall maintain insurance without cost to the Owner. By removing the specifics of the insurance requirements, the changed text introduces ambiguity and makes it impossible to ensure that the insurance policies obtained by the Operator adequately protect Owner's interests, directly contradicting the intent of Section 9.22. The deletion of the terms effectively means Owner is not adequately protected by the insurance, which introduces uncertainty to the contradicted section. This fits the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
